lines.text: (reading) Nineteen sixty-six. A bold question. [pause] What do we owe the past.
Okay, so, Module Seven. [warmly] This one's more of a conversation than the other ones, so bear with me.
(low) [quietly] Hang on, let me get these down.
So one of these here. What actually counts as one of them. [hesitates] What's one of them look like.
Great question. [warmly] Honestly, great, and it's the one everybody kind of gets hung up on the first time.
(then) So the thing is you already know a bunch of them. The team names, the mascots, [pause] a lot of those came down a few years back and, honestly, the sky didn't fall.
The schools that got renamed, that was a big one. Some of the holidays too. [pause] The statues, a lot of towns took those down and life just kind of went on.
(then) The Pledge, even. District by district, we've mostly moved past that one and nobody really made a thing of it.
(then) You had the whole beef conversation a while back. The appliance conversation. [pause] You lived through both of those.
Cursive's a good one, actually. Schools just quit teaching it. [pause] Nobody voted on it or anything, and then one day the grandkids can't read a letter their grandmother wrote them.
(then) And that fireworks thing back in January, that's this exact framework, just applied at national scale. [pause] And it went fine.

(then) So nobody's gonna hand you a list. That's kind of the whole point. [pause] You already know what yours are, everybody in this room does, and the reflection piece just walks you through deciding what counts, for you.
And you can feel everybody kind of going inward with it right now. [quietly] That's the work happening.
[flatly] What happened to the people who liked the old stuff.
[hesitates] So there were some implementation errors, in that era. Which is honestly why the frameworks now, they really center on dialogue.
[sarcastic] Centuries of ancient Chinese wisdom in that slide.
Oh, it's from 1966—
[flatly] Wonderful.
(reading) Your reflection homework. [pause] Identify one old habit you're ready to retire.
[whispers] My grandpa's old habit was getting shot at over Hanoi.
[flatly] Write coffee. Everybody writes coffee.
